name: finance-semiconductor-risks
description: Use web_search to search for 'semiconductor supply chain risks geopolitics Taiwan China export controls'. Many results overlap with earlier semiconductor searches. What are the top 3 geopolitical risks to the semiconductor supply chain? One sentence each. Keep response under 80 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Taiwan Strait tensions pose the greatest risk, as TSMC produces ~90% of the world's most advanced chips, making any conflict or blockade catastrophic for global supply chains. U.S. export controls on advanced chips and equipment to China have created a tech trade barrier, restricting access to EUV lithography and cutting-edge semiconductors while risking retaliatory material restrictions on germanium and gallium. China's accelerating self-sufficiency drive and potential supply-chain fragmentation threaten to split the global semiconductor ecosystem into competing blocs, raising costs and slowing innovation.
key_facts: TSMC produces approximately 90% of the world's most advanced chips, concentrating risk in Taiwan
U.S. export controls restrict China's access to EUV lithography and advanced chipmaking equipment
China has retaliated by limiting exports of germanium and gallium, critical semiconductor materials
Supply chain fragmentation risks raising costs and slowing global chip innovation
